metadata.description: Form 1120-S (U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation) intake questions. Covers entity info, officers, shareholders, income, deductions, balance sheet, reconciliation, distributions, and K-1 allocations.

sections.title: Entity Information
sections.description: Basic S Corporation identification and formation details

questions.text: Legal name of the S Corporation
Employer Identification Number (EIN)
Date of incorporation
State of incorporation
Principal business address
Principal business activity description
questions.helpText: Describe the primary business activity (e.g., 'Software Development', 'Real Estate Management')

questions.text: Business activity code (NAICS)
questions.helpText: 6-digit code from the Principal Business Activity Codes list

questions.text: Date S election became effective
questions.helpText: The date the IRS approved the S election (Form 2553)

questions.text: Does the corporation use a fiscal year end other than December 31?
Fiscal year end date
Business phone number
Total number of shareholders at end of tax year
Did the corporation's S election status change or terminate during the tax year?

sections.title: Officer Information
sections.description: Details for each corporate officer. Complete for all officers who served during the tax year.

questions.text: Officer's full name
Officer's title
questions.helpText: e.g., President, Vice President, Secretary, Treasurer, CEO, CFO

questions.text: Officer's Social Security Number
Officer's compensation for the tax year
questions.helpText: Total W-2 wages paid to this officer during the tax year

questions.text: Ownership percentage
Percentage of time devoted to business
questions.helpText: Estimated percentage of working time devoted to the corporation

questions.text: Officer's residential address
Is this officer also a shareholder?
Did this officer receive any fringe benefits (health insurance, vehicle use, etc.)?
Total value of fringe benefits provided

sections.title: Shareholder Information
sections.description: Details for each shareholder of the S Corporation. Complete for all shareholders who held stock at any time during the tax year.

questions.text: Shareholder's full name (or entity name)
Is this shareholder an individual or an entity?
Shareholder's Social Security Number
Shareholder's EIN
Shareholder's address
Ownership percentage
Type of stock held
Number of shares held at beginning of year
Number of shares held at end of year
Did any shares change hands during the year (purchases, sales, transfers)?
Does the shareholder have any loans to the corporation?
Balance of loans from shareholder to corporation
Does the corporation have any loans to the shareholder?
Balance of loans from corporation to shareholder

sections.title: Accounting Method & Tax Year
sections.description: Accounting method and tax year information for the S Corporation

questions.text: What accounting method does the corporation use?
Describe the accounting method
Does the corporation use a calendar year (January 1 - December 31)?
Fiscal year beginning date
Fiscal year ending date
Did the corporation change its accounting method during the tax year?
Was Form 3115 (Application for Change in Accounting Method) filed?
Section 481(a) adjustment amount, if any
questions.helpText: The adjustment required when changing accounting methods

sections.title: Gross Receipts / Income
sections.description: Total income items reported on Form 1120-S page 1

questions.text: Gross receipts or sales
questions.helpText: Total revenue from the sale of goods or services before any deductions

questions.text: Did the corporation have any returns and allowances?
Total returns and allowances
Does the corporation sell products or have inventory?
questions.helpText: If yes, Cost of Goods Sold section will need to be completed

questions.text: Did the corporation have net gain or loss from Form 4797 (sale of business property)?
Net gain (or loss) from Form 4797
Did the corporation have any other income (interest, rents, royalties, etc.)?
Total other income
Description of other income
Did the corporation receive any 1099 forms?
Please upload all 1099 forms received

sections.title: Cost of Goods Sold
sections.description: For S Corporations that sell products or maintain inventory (Schedule A of Form 1120-S)

questions.text: What inventory valuation method does the corporation use?
Describe the inventory valuation method
Was there any change in determining quantities, costs, or valuations between opening and closing inventory?
Inventory at beginning of year
Purchases
Cost of labor
Additional Section 263A costs
questions.helpText: Uniform capitalization (UNICAP) costs required to be included in inventory under Section 263A

questions.text: Other costs
Description of other costs
Inventory at end of year
Does the corporation use the LIFO inventory method?

sections.title: Deductions
sections.description: Deductible business expenses reported on Form 1120-S page 1 (Lines 7-19)

questions.text: Total officer compensation
questions.helpText: Combined W-2 compensation for all corporate officers

questions.text: Salaries and wages (non-officer employees)
Repairs and maintenance
Bad debts
questions.helpText: Debts owed to the corporation that became worthless during the year

questions.text: Rents paid
Taxes and licenses
questions.helpText: State and local taxes, payroll taxes, business licenses, and permits (not federal income tax)

questions.text: Interest expense
Depreciation (from Form 4562, if required)
Depletion (other than oil and gas)
Advertising expenses
Pension, profit-sharing, and other qualified retirement plan contributions
Employee benefit programs (health insurance, life insurance, dependent care, etc.)
Did the corporation have any other deductions not listed above?
Description of other deductions
Amount of other deduction

sections.title: Depreciation & Asset Dispositions
sections.description: Form 4562 depreciation and amortization, plus asset sales (Form 4797)

questions.text: Did the corporation purchase any new business assets during the tax year?
Description of new asset
Date new asset was placed in service
Cost or other basis of new asset
Business use percentage of new asset
Does the corporation wish to elect Section 179 expensing for any assets?
questions.helpText: Section 179 allows immediate expensing of qualifying property up to the annual limit

questions.text: Total Section 179 election amount
Does the corporation have existing assets from prior years still being depreciated?
Please upload the depreciation schedule from the prior year return
Did the corporation dispose of (sell, trade, abandon, or involuntarily convert) any business assets during the year?
Description of disposed asset
Date the asset was disposed of
Sales price or amount realized on disposition

sections.title: Balance Sheet (Schedule L)
sections.description: Beginning and end of year balance sheet. Required if total receipts and total assets are both $250,000 or more.

questions.text: Cash and cash equivalents — beginning of year
Cash and cash equivalents — end of year
Accounts receivable (net) — beginning of year
Accounts receivable (net) — end of year
Inventories — beginning of year
Inventories — end of year
Other current assets — beginning of year
Other current assets — end of year
Loans to shareholders — beginning of year
Loans to shareholders — end of year
Other assets (net of depreciation) — beginning of year
questions.helpText: Includes fixed assets, intangibles, and other long-term assets net of accumulated depreciation

questions.text: Other assets (net of depreciation) — end of year
Accounts payable — beginning of year
Accounts payable — end of year
Other current liabilities — beginning of year
Other current liabilities — end of year
Loans from shareholders — beginning of year
Loans from shareholders — end of year
Other liabilities — beginning of year
Other liabilities — end of year
Capital stock — beginning of year
Capital stock — end of year
Additional paid-in capital — beginning of year
Additional paid-in capital — end of year
Retained earnings — beginning of year
Retained earnings — end of year
Please upload the year-end balance sheet or trial balance from your accounting system

sections.title: Book-to-Tax Reconciliation (Schedule M-1)
sections.description: Reconciliation of income per books with income per return. Required when total assets are $250,000 or more.

questions.text: Net income (loss) per books
Income included on Schedule K not recorded on books this year
questions.helpText: e.g., tax-exempt interest recorded on the return but not in book income

questions.text: Expenses recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
questions.helpText: e.g., book depreciation exceeding tax depreciation, non-deductible expenses

questions.text: Description of book expenses not deducted on return
Income recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
questions.helpText: e.g., tax-exempt interest, life insurance proceeds

questions.text: Deductions included on Schedule K not charged against books this year
questions.helpText: e.g., tax depreciation exceeding book depreciation

questions.text: Description of deductions on return not on books
Were there any meals or entertainment expenses subject to limitation?
Total meals and entertainment per books
Non-deductible portion of meals and entertainment

sections.title: Distributions & AAA (Schedule M-2)
sections.description: Analysis of Accumulated Adjustments Account (AAA), Other Adjustments Account (OAA), and Shareholders' Undistributed Taxable Income Previously Taxed (PTI)

questions.text: Accumulated Adjustments Account (AAA) — balance at beginning of year
Ordinary income added to AAA
questions.helpText: Net ordinary income from page 1 of Form 1120-S

questions.text: Other additions to AAA
questions.helpText: Other income and gain items from Schedule K (excluding tax-exempt income)

questions.text: Loss and deduction items reducing AAA
Distributions from AAA
questions.helpText: Cash and property distributions made to shareholders from AAA

questions.text: Other reductions to AAA
Other Adjustments Account (OAA) — balance at beginning of year
questions.helpText: Tax-exempt income and related expenses tracked separately

questions.text: Additions to OAA (tax-exempt income)
Reductions to OAA (non-deductible expenses related to tax-exempt income)
Distributions from OAA
Shareholders' undistributed taxable income previously taxed (PTI) — beginning balance
questions.helpText: Only applies to S Corps with accumulated E&P from C Corp years

questions.text: Were any property distributions (non-cash) made to shareholders?
Fair market value of property distributed
Does the corporation have accumulated earnings and profits (E&P) from prior C Corporation years?

sections.title: Schedule K-1 Allocation
sections.description: How income, loss, deductions, and credits are allocated among shareholders, including Section 199A QBI information

questions.text: How are income, loss, deductions, and credits allocated among shareholders?

options.label: Pro-rata based on stock ownership percentage

questions.text: Were there any changes in share ownership during the year?
Did the corporation and all affected shareholders elect to use the specific accounting method (closing of the books) for mid-year changes?
Does the corporation have qualified business income (QBI) under Section 199A?
questions.helpText: QBI is generally net income from a qualified trade or business. Certain service businesses (SSTBs) may be limited.

questions.text: Is this business a specified service trade or business (SSTB)?
questions.helpText: SSTBs include health, law, accounting, actuarial, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage, and businesses where the principal asset is the reputation or skill of employees

questions.text: Total W-2 wages paid by the qualified business
questions.helpText: Used for the W-2 wage limitation on the Section 199A deduction

questions.text: Unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition (UBIA) of qualified property
questions.helpText: The original cost basis of tangible depreciable property used in the business, still within the applicable recovery period

questions.text: Does the corporation have any separately stated items that must be reported on K-1 (e.g., charitable contributions, Section 1231 gains, foreign transactions)?
Total charitable contributions made by the corporation
Did the corporation have any tax-exempt income?
Total tax-exempt income
Non-deductible expenses (other than distributions)
questions.helpText: e.g., 50% of meals, penalties, fines, political contributions, life insurance premiums on officers

sections.title: State Filing Requirements
sections.description: State income tax and franchise tax filing obligations for the S Corporation

questions.text: Include shared state-nexus questions
Does the corporation file composite returns on behalf of non-resident shareholders in any state?
questions.helpText: Some states require or allow S Corps to file a composite return and pay tax on behalf of non-resident shareholders

questions.text: Select states where composite returns are filed
Is the corporation subject to any state-level entity tax or franchise tax?
questions.helpText: Some states impose an entity-level tax on S Corporations (e.g., California franchise tax, New York fixed dollar minimum)

questions.text: Select states where entity-level or franchise tax applies
Did the corporation elect to pay a pass-through entity tax (PTET) in any state?
questions.helpText: Many states now allow S Corps to elect to pay an entity-level tax that provides a SALT cap workaround for shareholders

questions.text: Select states where PTET election was made
Total PTET payments made during the tax year
Were state estimated tax payments made during the year?
Please upload documentation of state estimated tax payments
